description_long: This is a vintage box-style television with a woodgrain-patterned exterior casing and a large convex glass screen dominating the front. The right side of the front panel features two prominent rotary dials for channel and volume control, as well as a speaker grille below them. The television's frame is bordered by a thick, metallic bezel, and the overall design is rectangular with sharp edges. The back panel is matte black with subtle venting and a recessed area likely for cable connections. The sides and top are finished in a dark wood veneer, giving it a classic, retro appearance. This type of television would commonly be found in older urban homes, garages, or even as discarded electronics on city streets.
description: A retro box-style television with a woodgrain exterior, large convex glass screen, and rotary dials on the front right. The design features a thick metallic bezel and a matte black back panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large convex glass screen, two rotary dials, speaker grille, and thick metallic bezel.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the dark woodgrain finish and the protruding dials on the front edge.
description_view_2: Rear view with a matte black panel, subtle venting, and a recessed area likely for cable connections.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the continuous woodgrain finish and the protruding dials on the front edge.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick metallic bezel, silver color, rectangular, with a woodgrain border and sharp corners.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two large rotary dials and a button cluster on the front right edge, arranged vertically.
